title: RepoPairCard Component
content:
The `RepoPairCard` component is responsible for displaying and managing a pair of connected GitHub repositories within the DocMed dashboard. It allows users to view the connection status, name, and configuration of a target repository and its associated documentation repository. ## Purpose This component serves as a visual representation of a repository pair, enabling users to: - View the status of the connection (ready, incomplete, not configured). - Edit the name of the repository pair. - Manage the connection to the target and documentation repositories. - Select or change the connected GitHub repositories. - Delete the repository pair. ## Key Components and Functionality ### `getPairStatus(pair: RepoPair): PairStatus` A helper function that determines the status of a repository pair based on whether both target and documentation repositories are configured. - `ready`: Both repositories are configured. - `incomplete`: One of the repositories is configured, but the other is not. - `not_configured`: Neither repository is configured. ### `StatusBadge` Component Displays a visual badge indicating the status of the repository pair, using different colors and icons for each status. ### `EditableName` Component Allows users to edit the name of the repository pair. It provides an inline editing interface with save and cancel options. ### `ConnectionSlot` Component A reusable component that renders a single repository connection slot (either 'Target Repo' or 'Doc Repo'). It displays: - The repository's title and connection status (including the GitHub login if connected). - A button to select or change the repository. - The currently selected repository (if any). - A description of the repository's role and the required permissions. - It integrates with `RepoPicker` and `ExistingAccountPicker` for repository and account selection. ### `RepoPairCard` (Main Component) - **Props**: `pair: RepoPair` - An object containing details about the repository pair. - **State**: Manages deletion confirmation (`confirmOpen`) and deletion progress (`deleting`). - **Functionality**: - Renders the `EditableName` and `StatusBadge` in the header. - Provides a button to delete the repository pair, with a confirmation modal. - Uses a grid layout to display two `ConnectionSlot` components for the target and documentation repositories. - Dynamically opens the `RepoPicker` or `ExistingAccountPicker` based on URL search parameters (`pick` and `pairId`) for immediate selection upon navigation. ## Usage Example ```tsx import { RepoPairCard } from '@/app/dashboard/integrations/_components/RepoPairCard'; import type { RepoPair } from '@/lib/repo-pairs'; const sampleRepoPair: RepoPair = { id: '123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000', name: 'My Project Docs', targetRepo: { repoFullName: 'owner/target-repo', githubLogin: 'owner', connected: true, }, docRepo: { repoFullName: 'owner/doc-repo', githubLogin: 'owner', connected: true, }, }; function MyDashboardPage() { return (
{/* Other dashboard components */}
); } ``` This component is typically used within a dashboard or settings page where users can manage their connected GitHub repositories for documentation synchronization.
